Road Name: FC Road (Fergusson College Road) & JM Road (Jangli Maharaj Road):

Goodluck Cafe Lane: The stretch from Goodluck Hotel to Suvarnasmruti Mangal Karyalaya has been declared a strict no-parking zone on both sides due to heavy resident complaints about double-parking and blocked gates.
akatdar Path Road (Deccan/Chaturshringi border): The 300-meter road from Chaitrali Co-Op Housing Society to Kshitij Bungalow is a no-parking zone on both sides.
Near Junctions and Bus Stands: Parking within 8 meters of major intersections like Khanduji Baba Chowk, Goodluck Chowk, or near the Deccan PMPML Bus Depot/Riverbed area is illegal.

The Riverbed Road: While parts are used for parking, parking haphazardly or leaving abandoned vehicles near the Kakasaheb Gadgil Bridge (Z-Bridge area) can result in nighttime sweeps by the traffic police.
Footpaths and Cycle Tracks: Any vehicle parked on or encroaching onto a pedestrian footpath or designated cycle track on FC/JM road will be towed immediately.
The District Court & PMC Metro Station Area: The entire periphery around the Pune District Court and the Pune Municipal Corporation (PMC) building is a zero-tolerance zone.

Modern College Road: The lane leading from Jansanchar Chowk to Modern College faces severe bottlenecks due to students and shoppers.

Juna Bazaar Area: The entire stretch between Juna Bazaar Chowk and Kumbharve Chowk is a designated permanent No-Parking zone

Chhatrapati Shivaji Market Yard Inner Connecting Roads: Parking commercial vehicles, tempos, or private cars on the internal thoroughfares (away from designated loading bays) is prohibited.

The Stretch between Maharashtra Warehousing Corporation Chowk and Utsav Chowk: This is a heavily monitored bottleneck corridor. Parking vehicles on the roadside along this entire stretch is permanently restricted
Flower Market (Phool Bazar) & Fruit Market Gate Perimeter: No unauthorized parking is permitted on the approach roads

Salisbury Park & Maharshi Nagar Connecting Roads: The primary avenues connecting Market Yard to Maharshi Nagar feature strict P1 / P2 (Odd / Even date) alternating parking rules for cars and two-wheelers.

Pune-Satara Road (Border Stretch): On the main thoroughfare from Dhankawadi / Balaji Nagar up toward Laxmi Narayan Chowk, parking any private four-wheeler or two-wheeler on the roadside is strictly prohibited.

Sahakarnagar Main Road (Near Commercial Clusters): The core avenue passing near popular local cafes, banks, and commercial establishments enforces strict temporary and permanent no-parking

The P1 / P2 Alternating System: Much like the core city areas, many internal residential and commercial cross-lanes in Tulshibagwale Colony, Walvekar Nagar, and Padmadarshan Society operate on an Odd/Even date system (P1/P2).

Taljai Hills Approach Roads: Parking on the direct approach curves and narrow entry pathways leading up to the Taljai Forest Reserve area is restricted,
School Perimeter Zones: Roads outside major academic institutions in the area have designated No Halting / No Parking

Pune-Bangalore Highway (NH-48 Bypass / Service Roads): Parking of heavy goods trucks, tempos, or private vehicles on both the main lanes and the service roads along the bypass (especially near Navale Bridge, Katraj Ghat approach, and toward Punawale/Wakad) is strictly prohibited.

Pune-Satara Road (Katraj Stretch): The main thoroughfare leading from Balaji Nagar/Dhankawadi up to Katraj Chowk is a dedicated mobility corridor where roadside parking of four-wheelers is permanently banned.
Katraj-Kondhwa Road: Due to the narrow, heavily congested nature of this connecting link, parking on either side of the road from Katraj Chowk toward Gokul Nagar and Rajas Society is prohibited.
Katraj Junction (Jedhe Chowk / Katraj Chowk Hub): The entire radius surrounding Katraj Chowk, the MSRTC Bus Stand entry/exit, and the PMPML bus depot is a strict No Halting / No Parking zone.

Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park (Katraj Zoo) Perimeter: On weekends and holidays, parking directly on the main Satara road outside the zoo gates is strictly banned.
No vehicles are allowed to park within 50 meters of major Chowk - Trimurti Chowk, Mahesh Society Chowk, and Dolphin Chowk.

Rajaram Bridge to Fun Time Multiplex Stretch: This is the most critical bottleneck on Sinhagad Road. Parking any private four-wheeler or two-wheeler on the main carriageway along this entire stretch is strictly banned
Abhiruchi Mall & Multiplex Perimeter: Parking on the main Sinhagad Road or on the immediate approach lanes outside the entrance/exit gates of Abhiruchi Mall is entirely prohibited.

Dhayari Phata Junction: The intersection where Dhayari Road meets Sinhagad Road is highly prone to gridlock. Roadside parking or halting for passengers/loading within this immediate junction is banned

No vehicles are allowed to park within 50 meters of major Chowk - Pu. La. Deshpande Udyan (Okayama Garden) Junction, Santosh Hall Chowk, Manikbaug Chowk, Vadgaon Phata / Katraj-Dehu Road Bypass Chowk (especially around the highway flyover entry/exit ramps where heavy vehicles merge)

MIT College Road (Rambaug Colony): The stretch leading to and directly outside the MIT WPU Campus is a strict zero-tolerance zone.

No vehicles are allowed to park within 50 meters of major Chowk - Nal Stop Junction (especially near the flyover ramps), Karve Putla Chowk, Chandni Chowk Approach Lanes (under the new multi-tier flyover system), Ashish Garden Chowk

Hadapsar Gadital (ST & PMPML Bus Stand Perimeter): The entire circle and approach margins surrounding the Gadital flyover, the state transport bus stand, and the local PMPML terminal are high-enforcement No Halting / No Parking zones.

Long-standing parking of tourist cabs, taxis, and employee vehicles on these roads is strictly banned
Main Commercial Road Margins: Haphazard roadside parking directly outside corporate parks, local food clusters, and transit corridors under the division is banned

Pune Railway Station Perimeter & Sasoon Road: Parking or unauthorized idling of private cars, auto-rickshaws, and cabs outside the main railway station entry/exit gates, the ST bus stand, or along Sasoon Road is completely banned.

Bund Garden Road (Main Spine): Roadside parking on the main lanes stretching from Yerwada Bridge (Sangamwadi side) past the Council Hall/Collector Office up to Jehangir Hospital is strictly prohibited

Council Hall & New Collector Office Perimeter: Due to administrative security and frequent VIP visits, the entire road boundary wrapping around the Council Hall, Divisional Commissioner's Office, and the New Collector Office complex enforces a strict No Halting / No Parking rule.

No vehicles are allowed to park within 50 meters of major Chowk - Sadhu Vaswani Chowk (Near Alpa Bachat Bhavan), Jehangir Hospital / Sancheti Corner Junction, GPO Chowk (General Post Office), Maldhakka Chowk, Blue Diamond Chowk (leading into Koregaon Park)

Famous Chowk (New Sangavi): Heavily monitored due to high pedestrian movement and narrow turning radii.

Shirode Road: Running from Sai Chowk, this stretch is strictly monitored for double-row parking.

Ankushrao Landge Auditorium Junction: Parking directly on the main road outside the auditorium complex during events is disallowed.

Old Pune-Mumbai Highway (Kasarwadi/Phugewadi border stretches): The long-standing statutory no-parking orders remain fully active on the main highway

Bhosari-Alandi Road: This stretch is subject to intense monitoring. While alternate day (P-1/P-2) parking signs exist on certain commercial pockets, parking anywhere near the newly constructed four-storey multi-level parking

Dehu Road Bazar / Main Market Lane: Roadside parallel or double-row parking by shoppers or shop owners along the primary bazaar stretch is prohibited.

Chikhali-Moshi Link Road (Industrial Stretch): Parallel or double-row parking near major commercial junctions or industrial units is strictly banned to keep the mixed transit lanes clear.
Sonawane Vasti & Pawar Vasti Main Access Lanes: These connecting lanes guide logistics into the heart of Chikhali; parking on the road margins is heavily penalized.
Dehu-Alandi Road (Chikhali Stretch): As a crucial arterial pilgrim and commuter highway corridor, leaving unattended private cars or delivery vehicles along the highway shoulders is illegal.

Chikhali-Akurdi Road / Spine Road Junction Flanks: Parking within 30 to 50 meters of this busy transition node is a direct towing violation.

Pune–Alandi Road (Dighi to Alandi Stretch): In coordination with the Alandi Municipal Council's anti-encroachment drives, the traffic police maintain a strict clear-carriageway mandate from Dehu Phata to Kale Colony. Dighi-Bhosari Road & Wadmukhwadi Stretches: Parking near commercial complexes or blocking the narrow shoulders on these connecting lines is prohibited.

Badha Chowk: Zero tolerance for roadside parking near this critical connection point on the highway margin.

Chande-Nande Road (Mahalunge Stretches): Unauthorized heavy commercial vehicles or private transport vans parked on the shoulders of this link road face direct enforcement.

High-Rise Society Frontages (e.g., Godrej, VTP, and Yashwin clusters): To maintain clear access for emergency and public transport, parking private cars or delivery fleets on the public approach streets outside these mega-township main gates is banned.

Near Baramati ST Stand: The immediate vicinity of the bus depot is a high-enforcement zone for public safety.

Market Areas (e.g., Main Bazaar Peth): Narrow streets in the city center often implement "One-Side Parking" or "No Parking" rules during peak business hours. 
School and Hospital Zones: Parking is prohibited within 50 meters of these institutions for safety reasons.

Look for Official Signage: The only legally binding "No Parking" zones are those marked with the standard circular blue sign featuring a red border and a red cross (or a single diagonal slash for "No Waiting").

Yellow/Red Curb Markings: Painted curbs indicate restricted parking. A solid yellow line typically denotes a "No Waiting" or loading-only zone, while red curbs usually indicate a permanent "No Stopping" area.

Daund Railway Junction & Station Road: As one of the busiest railway hubs in the region, the entire stretch leading up to Daund Junction is heavily regulated. Leaving a vehicle unattended on Station Road outside designated railway parking lots will result in immediate towing. 
Shivaji Chowk & Main Market Arteries: The areas radiating out from Shivaji Chowk (near the Daund Police Station) and the central market blocks are strictly "No Parking" zones to ensure the passage of local state transport (ST) buses and emergency vehicles.

Gopal Chowk to Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Chowk: Roadside parking is highly restricted on this busy commercial corridor. Local traffic police frequently run enforcement drives here to clear structural bottlenecks. 
National & State Highway Access Points: Connecting routes heading toward the Kurkumbh MIDC area or merging with major highways feature strict "No Halting" or "No Parking" rules on the shoulders to prevent accidents involving heavy multi-axle trucks.

The 15-Meter Intersection Rule: In alignment with Maharashtra Motor Vehicle Rules, parking within 15 meters of any major Chowk (junction), T-point, or narrow alleyway entrance in Daund is automatically treated as an obstruction and penalized. 
P1 / P2 Parallel Parking Rules: On several congested commercial streets, Daund utilizes an alternate-day parking system. Check local signage carefully—parking on the wrong side of the street according to the odd/even date will trigger an e-challan.

Raja Raghunathrao High School Road & ST Stand: The roads surrounding the central State Transport (ST) Bus Stand and leading towards the local state schools are high-priority clear zones. Parking on the roadside here causes heavy bus bottlenecks and is strictly penalized. 
Bhor Rajwada (Bhor Palace) Surroundings: As a major historical landmark and frequent movie shooting location, the roads and squares directly adjacent to the Bhor Rajwada maintain highly strict parking regulations to accommodate tourist foot traffic and tourist buses.

Main Market Line (Bazarpeth): The main commercial market lane is narrow. Leaving a four-wheeler parked on the main market stretch during daytime hours will result in immediate traffic police intervention. 
Bhor-Mahad Road (Pandharpur-Mahad Highway Section): The highway stretch passing through the municipal limits of Bhor is a continuous "No Halting" zone. Heavy passenger and transport vehicles are banned from parking on the highway shoulders.

Nasrapur Phata / Bhor-Kapurhol Link: If you are stopping near the highway junction points or the major arterial entry points into Bhor, parking within the immediate vicinity of the main chowks (crossroads) is completely prohibited.

Wagholi & Ahmednagar Road Corridor: As one of Haveli's heaviest traffic zones, roadside parking is strictly banned along the main Nagar Road stretch passing through Wagholi, especially near Baif Road, Tulapur Phata, and busy market intersections.

Sinhagad Road & Khadakwasla Stretch: The multi-lane roads passing through Kirkatwadi, Nanded Phata, and heading up towards the Khadakwasla Dam area are strict "No Halting" clearway zones. Parking along the main road shoulders—especially during weekends—triggers immediate towing.

Dhayari Phata & Narhe Corridor: The heavily congested approaches to Dhayari Phata, Narhe Road, and the surrounding industrial/residential alleys are strictly regulated. Leaving a vehicle unattended outside of marked shop boundaries will invite an e-challan.

Hadapsar-Fursungi-Loni Kalbhor Link: The major connecting state highways and internal roads running through Fursungi and towards Loni Kalbhor feature strict curbside restrictions to keep the lanes clear for heavy multi-axle freight transport.
The 15-Meter Intersection Rule: Parking within 15 meters of any busy Phata (junction), major T-point, or narrow highway merging ramp is automatically categorized as a traffic hazard and penalized.

Chakan-Talegaon Road & Chakan-Shikrapur Road: These are heavy industrial corridors. Roadside parking for any heavy vehicle or private car is strictly illegal along these routes. Leaving a vehicle on the shoulder causes severe bottlenecks for multi-axle industrial containers and will lead to an immediate fine.

Chakan Chowk (Manik Chowk / Ambethan Phata): The core junctions inside Chakan are completely strict "Clear Zones." Parking anywhere within 50 meters of these heavy bottlenecks is aggressively penalized.
Chakan Vegetable Market (Mahatma Phule Market): The surrounding approach lanes are highly congested. Parking is strictly limited to designated municipal or private spaces.

Pune-Nashik Highway ($NH-60$) Town Stretch: The section of the highway that cuts directly through Rajgurunagar acts as a major choke point. Parking on the highway shoulders or near the service roads is strictly prohibited.

Rajgurunagar ST Bus Stand & Market Line (Bazarpeth): The arterial roads leading to the central state transport bus stand and the main market are narrow. Four-wheelers are routinely barred from long-term curbside parking here.
Tindhar Chowk & Hanuman Nagar Intersections: These central city junctions undergo strict manual enforcement by traffic wardens daily.

Pradakshina Road: As a major holy path circling the Sant Dnyaneshwar Samadhi Mandir, the entire length of Pradakshina Road is a permanent No Parking and No Halting zone. Vehicles parked here are immediately towed to keep the paths clear for massive crowds of pilgrims.
Alandi Bus Stand & Indrayani River Bridge Approaches: The narrow entry bridges and the areas surrounding the local ST Stand feature strict tow-away zones.
Alephata Chowk ($NH-60$ and $NH-222$ Intersection): As one of the busiest highway crossroads in Pune district, parking within 50 meters of the main intersection is completely prohibited.

Alephata MSRTC Bus Stand Perimeter: The local traffic division and state transport administration strictly enforce a 200-meter No-Parking and Clearway Zone around the entire bus station. Private cars, tourist jeeps, and unauthorized private luxury buses parked on the curbs here are aggressively fined and towed to prevent heavy traffic bottlenecks.

Shivneri Fort Road Approach: The main approach roads climbing toward the base of Shivneri Fort feature strict no-parking restrictions, especially on weekends and public holidays. Visitors must use designated tourist parking bays.

Junnabazar / Main Bazarpeth Market: The historical market streets inside Junnar city are incredibly narrow. Four-wheelers are heavily restricted from curbside parking during business hours. 
Junnar ST Stand & Tahsil Office Road: Roadside parking outside the local government administrative offices and the state transport depot is illegal. Vehicles must use the allocated municipal utility parking slots.

Pune-Nashik Highway ($NH-60$) Town Stretch: The busy market stretches where the highway cuts through Narayangaon are continuous "No Halting" zones. Parking on highway shoulders to access local shops causes heavy accidents and triggers instant electronic fines.Narayangaon ST Stand & Primary Market Link: The local traffic wardens strictly clear vehicles that obstruct the turning radius of large state transport buses.

Shikrapur Chowk (Chakan-Shikrapur Flyover Zone): This is one of the worst traffic bottlenecks in the district. Roadside parking for any car, truck, or multi-axle carrier is strictly banned within several hundred meters of this main junction. Interceptor vans heavily penalize violators here. 
Kondhapuri & Perne Phata: As major connecting points feeding industrial traffic onto the highway, parking on the shoulders or service roads is an automatic violation.
Shirur Town Highway Stretch: The segment of the highway cutting directly past Shirur town is a strict No Halting clearway zone to keep long-distance transit moving smoothly.

MahaGanapati Temple Approach Roads: As a major Ashtavinayak temple destination, the immediate lanes leading to the temple are permanent tow-away zones—especially on weekends, Angarki Chaturthi, and festival days. Visitors must strictly use the designated Shree Mahaganapati New Parking lots.

Ranjangaon MIDC Main Avenues: Roads moving through the industrial manufacturing zones restrict unauthorized curbside parking. Heavy container trucks waiting for company gate openings must utilize official truck terminals (Atharv Yard or MIDC Parking Spaces) rather than blocking the public corridors.

Shirur ST Bus Stand & Market Line (Bazarpeth): The roads radiating from the state transport bus depot and the narrow historic commercial lanes are heavily restricted. Four-wheelers are routinely barred from daytime street-side parking here.

Tehsildar / Police Station Road: Roadside parking outside the local government administrative blocks is illegal. Visitors are directed to marked municipal slots.

Bhushi Dam Road (Aamby Valley Road): This is the most critical traffic corridor during the monsoon. Parking or stopping your vehicle on the main road or its unpaved shoulders is strictly prohibited. The traffic division deploys a dedicated towing squad here, and unauthorized vehicles are cleared immediately.

Tiger Point & Lion's Point Access: The narrow, winding roads leading up to these vantage points are permanent No Halting and No Parking zones. Visitors must use the designated, barricaded parking bays at the viewpoints.
Khandala Ghat & Old Mumbai-Pune Highway ($NH-48$) Stretches: Parking on the sides of the old highway to take photos, look at the valley, or access small food stalls is completely illegal.

Lonavala Market Line (Bazarpeth): The primary commercial lanes near the city core are extremely narrow. Four-wheelers are prohibited from long-term curbside parking here during busy daylight hours to prevent total paralysis of local transit.

Lonavala Railway Station & ST Bus Stand Roads: The approaching roads to the transit hubs are strict clearway zones. Dropping off passengers is permitted briefly, but leaving a vehicle unattended will result in an immediate towing fine or an e-challan.

Tungarli & Valvan Lake Roads: The approach lanes to these popular resort areas feature strict roadside parking bans, especially where the street width shrinks.
Major Junctions & Intersections: Within 15 meters of any major intersection, including Koregaon Park Junction (near the petrol pump/Burning Ghat road entry) and ABC Farm Junction.

Near the Osho International Meditation Resort: The immediate perimeter surrounding the Osho Ashram lane and its main gates is heavily monitored with no static vehicle parking allowed on the main thoroughfare.

Narrow Connecting Lanes & By-Lanes: Several narrow internal lanes connecting North and South Main Roads are marked as "No Parking" zones on both sides to ensure emergency vehicles and local traffic can pass without gridlock.

Public Utility & Safety Zones: Areas directly in front of hospitals, ambulance bays, school gates (such as near St. Felix or nearby educational centers), and the Koregaon Park Police Station / Mangaldas Police Chowki limits.

Shivaji Road (Select Stretches): The arterial stretch running through the heart of this division—especially near the Mandai (Mahatma Phule Mandai) area and around the Dagdusheth Halwai Ganpati Temple perimeter—is a zero-tolerance zone for unauthorized roadside parking.
Bajirao Road Junctions: Specifically around Shaniwar Wada leading down towards Appa Balwant Chowk (ABC) and the busy Tulshibaug entry points.
Subhash Nagar Road & T-Junctions: The highly commercialized turns near Subhash Nagar and the lanes feeding into the main wholesale markets.
Around Khadak Police Station: The immediate perimeter and access roads around the Khadak Police Station/Chowki itself are strictly designated for official police or emergency vehicles only.
Footpaths and Pedestrian Plazas: Due to severe crowding, parking two-wheelers on the sidewalks of Laxmi Road, Bajirao Road, or near Mandai results in heavy penalties.

Rasta Peth (Main Power House Road): Parking is strictly prohibited near the MSEDCL Power House junction and along the main arterial stretches to ensure continuous movement of utility and emergency vehicles.
Around KEM Hospital (Rasta Peth): The entire perimeter surrounding KEM Hospital, including the emergency ambulance bays and immediate approach lanes, is a strict No Parking / No Halting zone.
Apollo Talkies Chowk to its Connecting Arteries: The junctions branching out from this old landmark towards Rasta Peth and Somwar Peth are marked clear of any roadside vehicular storage.

Perimeter of Samarth Police Station: Roadways immediately surrounding the Samarth Police Station and the adjacent administrative chowkis are reserved strictly for law enforcement and emergency response vehicles.
Daruwala Pool to Apollo Cinema Road: This narrow, highly congested transit corridor connecting old Peth areas cannot accommodate static parking on either side.

The 15-Meter Junction Rule: Parking is strictly illegal within 15 meters of any major intersection or Chowk under Samarth jurisdiction to safeguard turn-radii for public transport and emergency vehicles.
Sidewalk Infringements: Due to heavily populated residential blocks in Mangalwar and Somwar Peth, parking two-wheelers on designated pedestrian footpaths invites severe structural fines.

M.G. Road Cross-Junctions (Walking Plazas): The immediate intersections near central commercial zones—specifically the Tryluck Restaurant Chowk and the area leading up to Hotel Aurora Towers—are strictly clear of unauthorized or double-parked vehicles.
The ISKCON Temple Perimeter: Stretches along the access ways around the local ISKCON temple and its adjacent inner lanes strictly prohibit roadside vehicle assembly to prevent regular traffic blocks.

Command Hospital & Military Medical Zones: Any approach corridor or thoroughfare heading towards the core Cantonment defense zones, military hospitals, and operational emergency access lanes are strictly protected No Parking / No Halting areas.

Around the Lashkar Police Station / Cantonment Chowkis: The immediate parameter walls and access roads surrounding the central Lashkar Police Station and local transit chowkis are entirely reserved for official defense and emergency machinery.
Footpaths & Pedestrian Shopping Plazas: Parking any two-wheeler or four-wheeler onto the sidewalks of M.G. Road or East Street is heavily fined due to high footfalls.

Salunke Vihar Road (Core Commercial Stretch): Roadside parking is highly restricted on the main stretches of Salunke Vihar Road, especially near major commercial clusters, food joints, and complex junctions where double-parking blocks the single lane of moving traffic.

Vitthal Rao Shivarkar Road: Stretches around major retail outlets (such as the Vishal Mega Mart area) are strictly monitored. Vehicles idling or parking randomly on the curbside face immediate action.

Samrat Ashok Road / Wanawadi Bazar Road: The tight, historical market streets of Wanawadi village/bazar are designated as zero-tolerance parking zones to ensure public transport buses and emergency vehicles can navigate without gridlock.

Around Armed Forces Medical College (AFMC) & Command Hospital Perimeter: Because Wanawadi borders extensive military medical establishments, the main arterial corridors and boundary walls leading toward AFMC and Command Hospital gates are strictly marked as No Parking / No Halting safety zones.

Azadnagar Access Corridors: The narrow entry and exit lanes feeding into Azadnagar have specific parking prohibitions enforced by the traffic branch to facilitate unobstructed passage for emergency vehicles like ambulances and fire brigades.
SRPF (State Reserve Police Force) Group 1 & 2 External Roads: The approach roads and external perimeters immediately flanking the SRPF campuses maintain strict security-driven No Parking protocols.
Lullanagar Chowk to Fakhri Hills Junction (400m Stretch): Roadside parking is banned on both sides of this busy corridor.

Jyoti Hotel Chowk to Fakhri Hills Junction (600m Stretch): Parking is strictly banned on one side of this heavy commercial stretch to halt double-parking from visitors frequenting nearby fast-food joints and banks.

Kausar Baug Masjid Road & Food Street Stretches: Because this lane is highly popular for evening food stalls, curbside parking is heavily restricted to prevent complete gridlock during evening peak hours.

Mithanagar Lane & Shantai Bhaji Mandai Entry Points: The access roads near the local vegetable market hubs are designated zero-tolerance zones to keep public transit and emergency vehicles moving freely.

Around Kondhwa Police Station / Local Chowkis: Road networks directly flanking the central police station precinct and surrounding administrative traffic chowkis are entirely reserved for official security machinery.

Sinhagad Road (Arterial Stretches): Parking is strictly prohibited on the main road shoulder near highly clogged sections, especially around Dandekar Bridge Chowk, Pan Mala, and the approach lanes to the Savarkar Flyover.

Mitra Mandal Chowk to Bajaj Statue Area: The entire thoroughfare running from Jedhe Chowk/Swargate boundary down through Mitra Mandal Chowk toward the Bajaj Statue is a zero-tolerance zone for roadside parking.
Anand Mangal Karyalaya to Dadawadi Jain Temple Corner: This heavily traversed inner link is kept completely clear of parked vehicles to prevent local bottlenecks.

Golvalkar Guruji Path (Junction Perimeters): While localized parking exists on parts of this street, parking is explicitly banned within 50 meters of Gajanan Maharaj Chowk on all four intersecting approaches.
Perimeter of Ramkrishna Math (Sinhagad Road): The immediate roadway facing the main gates of the Ramkrishna Math precinct is an active No Parking / No Halting zone.

Parvati Hill Base & Access Steps: Due to tourist and devotee crowds, the immediate narrow access lanes and winding roads around the lower steps of Parvati Hill maintain strict security-driven parking prohibitions.

Katraj Chowk Perimeter (Within 50 Meters): As one of South Pune's heaviest traffic bottlenecks, parking or halting vehicles within 50 meters of Katraj Chowk—extending towards the Katraj ST Stand, the PMPML bus depot, and the Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park (Katraj Zoo) main gate—is strictly banned.

Bharati Vidyapeeth University Main Campus Road: The thoroughfare directly outside the primary college gates (Katraj-Dehu Road Bypass side and internal link approach road) is a zero-tolerance zone for roadside parking. Vehicles idling on the main shoulder block student transit buses.
Katraj-Dehu Road Bypass (NH-48) Service Roads: Parking is prohibited on the service lanes near Ambegaon Budruk and the Navale Bridge stretch falling under this jurisdiction.

Around Dhankawadi Police Station & Local Chowkis: Roadways directly lining the Dhankawadi Police Station, the Bharati Vidyapeeth Police Station, and local traffic chowkis are entirely reserved for official police machinery and emergency response vehicles.

Trimurti Chowk to Last Bus Stop Road (Dhankawadi): The commercial curbsides along this narrow arterial lane are highly restricted to prevent complete gridlock during afternoon and evening market hours.

Karve Road (Main Arterial Stretches): Due to the high volume of traffic and the presence of major commercial properties and institutions, roadside parking is strictly banned on both sides of Karve Road within this jurisdiction.

Alankar Pool Road (Near Alankar Police Station): The main approach road and narrow lanes leading up to the Alankar Police Station precinct must remain entirely clear of unauthorized or stagnant public vehicles.

Deenanath Mangeshkar Hospital Proximity: The immediate approach corridors, emergency vehicle entrance/exit gates, and peripheral boundary walls of Deenanath Mangeshkar Hospital (Erandwane) are strict No Parking / No Halting emergency zones.

Rajaram Bridge to Karve Nagar Chowk Corridor: Roadside parking or temporary idling along this highly traveled arterial connection is strictly prohibited on both shoulders to avoid massive peak-hour spillover bottlenecks.

Near Cummins College Road (Select Junctions): The approach areas and intersections flanking Maharshi Karve Stree Shikshan Samstha (Cummins College) and its nearby coaching hubs maintain localized zero-tolerance parking zones to safeguard pedestrian movement.

The 15-Meter Junction Clear Rule: Parking is strictly illegal within 15 meters of any major intersecting Chowk (such as Karve Nagar Chowk or intersections near Cummins College) to preserve safe turning radii for PMPML buses.
Sidewalk Obstructions: Given the high density of student and senior citizen foot traffic in Karve Nagar and Erandwane, two-wheelers parked on pedestrian footpaths face instant penalties.

NDA Road (Main Market Stretches): Roadside parking is strictly prohibited on both shoulders along the core commercial stretch of Uttam Nagar and Shivane markets, where double-parking instantly bottlenecks commuter traffic.

NDA Khadakwasla Police Station Perimeter: The thoroughfare directly in front of and surrounding the NDA Khadakwasla Police Station (situated near Sanjivani Hospital) is an active zero-tolerance zone reserved entirely for law enforcement and emergency vehicles.
Sanjivani Hospital Approach & Ambulance Bays: The immediate roadside area flanking Sanjivani Hospital and nearby medical clinics must remain completely unobstructed to safeguard emergency access.

Deshmukhwadi and Ganpati Matha Chowk Intersections: No parking or heavy vehicle idling is permitted within 15 meters of these crucial junctions connecting internal village pockets to the main highway.
Ahilyadevi Holkar Chowk: The immediate vicinity of this prominent junction is highly monitored by traffic wardens to ensure smooth turning radii for public PMPML transit buses.

No Heavy Vehicle Halting: Heavy commercial transport, cement mixers, and loading trucks are entirely prohibited from parking, idling, or loading/unloading on the main shoulders of NDA Road between 6:00 AM and 11:00 PM.

Airport Road (New Airport Terminal Core Corridors): Roadside parking, extended waiting, or staging on the main shoulders of Airport Road and the access stretches directly flanking Terminal 1 and Terminal 2 is strictly banned.

Symbiosis International School & College Roads: The approach avenues and intersections outside Symbiosis in Viman Nagar are strictly marked as No Parking. Double-parking by private vehicles or auto-rickshaws during morning drop-off and afternoon pick-up times is heavily penalized.

Phoenix Marketcity Perimeter (Nagar Road Approach): The service road and immediate peripheral lanes directly outside Phoenix Marketcity Mall are zero-tolerance areas. Vehicles idling on the street to avoid mall parking charges are immediately towed.

Vimantal Police Station to Sanjay Park Lanes: Road networks immediately surrounding the Vimantal Police Control Room/Chowki and the narrow corridors running through Sanjay Park must remain unobstructed for patrol vehicles and airport emergency responders.
Datta Mandir Chowk & Ganpati Chowk Intersections: No parking or commercial loading/unloading is permitted within 15 meters of these major Viman Nagar junctions to prevent traffic bottlenecks.
Sidewalk Infringements: Parking two-wheelers on the paved footpaths of Viman Nagar results in immediate tyre-locking and a standard municipal safety penalty.
Bus Bays & Auto-Stands: Halting private vehicles within 15 meters of designated PMPML bus stops or authorized auto-rickshaw stands across this division is strictly illegal.

Vishrantwadi Chowk Intersection (50-Meter Radius): Parking, halting, or staging any private vehicle, auto-rickshaw, or private travel bus within 50 meters of the central five-arm junction is completely prohibited.

Alandi Road (Chowk to Phule Nagar Stretch): The main roadside shoulders of Alandi Road are strict zero-tolerance zones. This is highly monitored due to high-frequency PMPML transit bus routes moving towards northern suburbs.

Tingre Nagar Road (Main Approach): Roadside parking is banned on both sides of the main avenue heading from Vishrantwadi Chowk toward Tingre Nagar, especially near commercial commercial blocks where double-parking instantly blocks traffic.
Dhanori Road Corner & Slopes: The initial bottleneck stretch where the road ascends from the junction toward Dhanori is designated as a strict clear zone.

Perimeter of Vishrantwadi Police Station & Chowki: The roadways and access paths directly flanking the local police station and traffic administration hub are strictly reserved for official emergency machinery.

Bus Stops & Public Transport Transit Bays: Parking private vehicles within 15 meters of designated PMPML bus shelters across Alandi Road or Dhanori Road is heavily penalized by on-duty traffic wardens.
Flyover Approach Ramps: Halting or parking a vehicle anywhere near the start or exit ramps of the Vishrantwadi flyover network is strictly forbidden.

Elphinstone Road (Main Arterial Stretch): Roadside parking or temporary staging is completely banned on both sides of this major avenue. This restriction ensures that military convoys and emergency hospital transit remain entirely unobstructed.

Khadki Bazaar Core Market Roads: The internal narrow pathways running through Khadki Bazaar are zero-tolerance zones. Double-parking by commercial transport vehicles or shoppers here is heavily targeted during peak afternoon and evening trade hours.

Around Khadki Railway Station Access Roads: The immediate station approach lines, pick-up terminals, and peripheral walls must be kept clear of unauthorized parking to avoid blocking local PMPML bus routes and commuter drop-offs.

Aundh Road - Khadki Cantonment Stretches: Roadside parking is highly restricted on the main shoulders of Aundh Road falling within KCB limits, especially near dense residential societies and schools (such as near St. Thomas Public School layouts).

Perimeter of Ammunition Factory & Military Gates: The entire peripheral roadway flanking the Khadki Ammunition Factory walls, defense depots, and operational camp gates are designated secure No Parking / No Halting areas.
Near Khadki Police Station & Traffic Chowki: Road networks immediately surrounding the Khadki Police Station and local transit chowkis are explicitly reserved for law enforcement machinery.

Sidewalk Obstructions: Following strict Cantonment civic rules, parking any two-wheeler or four-wheeler on pedestrian footpaths across Khadki invites rapid towing and additional administrative penalties.

Bus Stops & Public Transport Transit Bays: Halting private vehicles within 20 meters prior to and 15 meters after designated PMPML bus shelters is strictly prohibited to guarantee unobstructed public transit maneuvers.

Bhosale Nagar Main Road (Select Stretches): To prevent morning and evening transit blocks, roadside parking is strictly restricted on the main spine of Bhosale Nagar, especially at the entry-exit necks that interface with Ganeshkhind Road.

Ganeshkhind Road (E-Square to University Flyover): Due to ongoing infrastructure projects, flyovers, and metro construction routes, the entire main road shoulder on both sides is a absolute No Parking / No Halting zone.

Senapati Bapat (SB) Road (Commercial Frontages): Parking parallel to the main road outside major commercial centers like the ICC Trade Tower, Pavilion Mall perimeter, and corporate offices is heavily restricted.
University (SPPU) Circle Boundary: Staging or parking private buses, tourist vehicles, or private vehicles within 50 meters of the central University Circle junction is strictly prohibited.

Perimeter of Chaturshringi Police Station & Raj Bhavan: The approach paths directly flanking the Chaturshringi Police Station (opposite Raj Bhavan gates) and the immediate administrative chowkis are reserved entirely for law enforcement and official security machinery.

The 15-Meter Junction Intersection Rule: Parking is strictly illegal within 15 meters of any key intersection (such as the crossroads connecting Bhosale Nagar to Range Hills or deep Erandwane links) to safeguard turning clearances for local municipal PMPML transit buses.
